steps.title: Set the equations equal
steps.content:
Both equations equal $y$, so set the right sides equal.
$$3x=x^2-28$$
$$x^2-3x-28=0$$

steps.title: Factor the quadratic
steps.content:
Factor the equation.
$$x^2-3x-28=(x-7)(x+4)$$
So the possible $x$-values are $7$ and $-4$.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graph the two equations and read the intersection with positive $x$. The positive intersection gives the needed value. The answer is 7.
